_**Agent (default)**_

I found **4 domain aggregates** (`BirthDeclarationCase`, `ChangeOfAddressCase`, `RegistrationCase`, `DocumentRequestCase`) that share not only the three properties but also almost identical methods: `Claim`, `ReleaseLock`, `EnsureEditableBy`, `IsLockedTo`, `IsLockedToAnother`. The only meaningful difference is the **exception type** each case throws (and minor null-check style), which impacts how we factor it out cleanly.

_**Agent (default)**_

Perfect—composition + an exception-factory hook is the cleanest DDD-friendly extraction here, and it avoids locking these aggregates into a brittle inheritance chain. Next I’ll draft a concrete refactor plan: new shared type location/API, how each case adopts it, and where the web layer pages/components will need trivial adjustments (if any).

Parameters:

````json
{
  "name": "Extract officer lock/claim",
  "overview": "Extract duplicated claim/lock behavior (AssignedOfficerId/LockedByOfficerId/LockedAt + related methods) from multiple Case aggregates into a shared composition-based type, while preserving each aggregate’s domain-specific exception type via an exception-factory hook.",
  "plan": "# Extract shared officer lock/claim behavior\n\n## What’s duplicated today\nThe following aggregates all repeat the same state + logic:\n- [src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/BirthDeclaration/BirthDeclarationCase.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/BirthDeclaration/BirthDeclarationCase.cs)\n- [src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/ChangeOfAddress/ChangeOfAddressCase.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/ChangeOfAddress/ChangeOfAddressCase.cs)\n- [src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/RegistrationCase.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/RegistrationCase.cs)\n- [src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/IdentityDocuments/DocumentRequestCase.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/IdentityDocuments/DocumentRequestCase.cs)\n\nShared members (example from `BirthDeclarationCase`):\n\n```21:129:/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/BirthDeclaration/BirthDeclarationCase.cs\npublic OfficerId? AssignedOfficerId { get; private set; }\n\npublic OfficerId? LockedByOfficerId { get; private set; }\n\npublic DateTimeOffset? LockedAt { get; private set; }\n\npublic CaseClaimResult Claim(OfficerId officer, DateTimeOffset at)\n{\n    if (LockedByOfficerId is { } locked \u0026\u0026 locked != officer)\n    {\n        throw new InvalidBirthDeclarationTransitionException(\n            \"This case is locked to another officer.\");\n    }\n\n    if (LockedByOfficerId == officer)\n    {\n        return CaseClaimResult.AlreadyHeld;\n    }\n\n    var hadAssignee = AssignedOfficerId is not null;\n    AssignedOfficerId = officer;\n    LockedByOfficerId = officer;\n    LockedAt = at;\n\n    return hadAssignee ? CaseClaimResult.Reclaimed : CaseClaimResult.NewlyClaimed;\n}\n\npublic void ReleaseLock(OfficerId officer)\n{\n    if (LockedByOfficerId != officer)\n    {\n        throw new InvalidBirthDeclarationTransitionException(\n            \"Only the officer holding the lock can release it.\");\n    }\n\n    LockedByOfficerId = null;\n    LockedAt = null;\n}\n\npublic void EnsureEditableBy(OfficerId officer, string operation)\n{\n    if (LockedByOfficerId is null)\n    {\n        throw new InvalidBirthDeclarationTransitionException(\n            $\"Cannot perform '{operation}' before the case is claimed.\");\n    }\n\n    if (LockedByOfficerId != officer)\n    {\n        throw new InvalidBirthDeclarationTransitionException(\n            $\"Cannot perform '{operation}' while the case is locked to another officer.\");\n    }\n}\n\npublic bool IsLockedTo(OfficerId officer) =\u003e LockedByOfficerId == officer;\n\npublic bool IsLockedToAnother(OfficerId officer) =\u003e\n    LockedByOfficerId is { } locked \u0026\u0026 locked != officer;\n```\n\nThe only real variation is the **exception type**:\n- `InvalidBirthDeclarationTransitionException`\n- `InvalidChangeOfAddressTransitionException`\n- `InvalidRegistrationTransitionException`\n- `InvalidDocumentRequestTransitionException`\n\n## Proposed design (composition)\nCreate a small domain helper that each aggregate *owns*:\n- **New type**: `OfficerLockState`\n  - **Location**: `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/` might be misleading; instead add a small cross-domain folder such as `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/CaseManagement/` (or similar) to avoid coupling the concept to one feature.\n  - **State**: `OfficerId? AssignedOfficerId`, `OfficerId? LockedByOfficerId`, `DateTimeOffset? LockedAt`\n  - **Behavior**: `Claim(...)`, `ReleaseLock(...)`, `EnsureEditableBy(...)`, `IsLockedTo(...)`, `IsLockedToAnother(...)`\n  - **Exception hook**: methods accept `Func\u003cstring, Exception\u003e` (or a small interface) so each aggregate can throw its own domain exception type while reusing the logic.\n\nEach case aggregate then becomes:\n- Keep public getters (`AssignedOfficerId`, `LockedByOfficerId`, `LockedAt`) but back them by `_lockState`.\n- Replace method bodies to delegate to `_lockState` with the aggregate’s exception factory.\n\nThis keeps:\n- **DDD invariants local** (cases still decide which exception to throw)\n- **No inheritance constraints**\n- **Minimal EF impact** (still private setters; we’ll ensure mapping still works—either by EF-owned type configuration or by keeping the same scalar properties and only factoring out behavior).\n\n## EF persistence strategy (choose safest)\nBecause these classes likely are EF entities (private ctor, private setters), we’ll use the least risky persistence approach:\n- **Option A (safest)**: keep the three scalar properties on the aggregate for EF mapping, but extract only the logic into a `static` helper or small service-like type (no EF-owned type mapping needed).\n- **Option B (cleaner model)**: model `_lockState` as an EF **owned entity type** and map its columns to the same table.\n\nIn implementation we’ll start with Option A unless there’s already a pattern of owned types in this Domain.\n\n## Changes to make\n- Add new shared type (or helper) under `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/...`.\n- Update the 4 `*Case` aggregates to delegate claim/lock operations.\n- Ensure public API remains identical so UI and application layer code doesn’t need changes.\n- Run build/tests to confirm no EF mapping regressions.\n\n## Impacted files (expected)\n- Domain:\n  - [src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/BirthDeclaration/BirthDeclarationCase.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/BirthDeclaration/BirthDeclarationCase.cs)\n  - [src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/ChangeOfAddress/ChangeOfAddressCase.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/ChangeOfAddress/ChangeOfAddressCase.cs)\n  - [src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/RegistrationCase.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/RegistrationCase.cs)\n  - [src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/IdentityDocuments/DocumentRequestCase.cs](src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/IdentityDocuments/DocumentRequestCase.cs)\n  - New file: `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/\u003cshared-folder\u003e/OfficerLockState.cs` (name/folder finalized during implementation)\n\n- Web layer: likely **no changes**, as long as property/method signatures remain the same. (We’ll re-check compilation for any reflection/serialization edge cases.)\n",
````

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Refining object properties**

I'm thinking it's better to keep the auto-properties and delegate logic to helper functions that manage the aggregate with an internal interface. I need to create this IOfficerLockable interface with private setters, but that complicates things. It seems I can't use private setters in interface implementation. So, the simplest solution is to create a helper class with generic methods. These methods can manipulate current values and provide new outputs while avoiding duplication, which leads to a cleaner design. I'll go ahead and implement this!
**Implementing case locking functionality**

I’m planning to create a static class called `OfficerCaseLocking`. The `Claim` method will take references for the officer IDs and the lock timestamp, but I realized I can't pass auto-properties as refs, which limits my approach. Instead, I consider using backing fields for the properties, although that introduces complexity. Alternatively, I could return new state from the method, allowing the caller to assign properties. The `ReleaseLock` method will also return new values, while `EnsureEditableBy` will just do checks. I'll set up a new file in the proper namespace too!
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

### What I changed
- **Added shared locking/claim logic** in `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/CaseManagement/OfficerCaseLocking.cs` (plus `OfficerLockState.cs` as a small wrapper type).
- **Refactored these aggregates to delegate** `Claim`, `ReleaseLock`, `EnsureEditableBy`, `IsLockedTo`, `IsLockedToAnother` while keeping:
  - the **same public properties** (`AssignedOfficerId`, `LockedByOfficerId`, `LockedAt`)
  - the **same return values**
  - the **same domain-specific exception types** via an exception-factory lambda
  - the **same EF mapping** (since the scalar properties remain unchanged)
  
Impacted files:
- `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/BirthDeclaration/BirthDeclarationCase.cs`
- `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/ChangeOfAddress/ChangeOfAddressCase.cs`
- `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/Registration/RegistrationCase.cs`
- `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/IdentityDocuments/DocumentRequestCase.cs`
- `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/CaseManagement/OfficerCaseLocking.cs`
- `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/CaseManagement/OfficerLockState.cs`

### Verification
- **`dotnet test` passed** (Domain, Integration, and E2E test suites all green).

---

_**User**_

my compiler says that there are many method not called for ex: ReleaseLock in @src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain/CaseManagement/OfficerLockState.cs - why is this method here then? 

---

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Evaluating code structure**

I need to explain that the wrapper type is currently unused, so I could suggest removing OfficerLockState entirely or simply suppressing it. The user is asking why this method exists, so I’ll respond by proposing we delete OfficerLockState and use a static helper instead. However, it’s essential to ask the user before making any changes since they didn't request modifications for now. Maybe I could propose some options briefly.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

`OfficerLockState` is **not actually used anywhere** right now (we refactored the 4 aggregates to call the static `OfficerCaseLocking.*` methods directly). Because of that, your compiler/analyzers correctly report methods like `OfficerLockState.ReleaseLock(...)` as **unused**.

So that method is there only because I added `OfficerLockState` as an *optional* “nice API” wrapper (so a case could do `state = state.ReleaseLock(...)` / `(state, result) = state.Claim(...)`). Since we didn’t adopt that pattern in the aggregates, it became dead code.

If you want, I can clean this up in one of two ways:
- **Remove `OfficerLockState.cs` entirely** (recommended; simplest, no unused warnings).
- **Switch the cases to use `OfficerLockState`** (keeps the wrapper, but means touching each case again).

Tell me which you prefer and I’ll apply it.

---

_**User**_

what do you recommend ? 

---

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Considering recommendations**

I need to give a concise recommendation on removing OfficerLockState and explain the reasoning behind it. I might offer to implement it as well. As for tools, I won't use them unless the user specifically asks for that. Since the focus is on clarity and directness, I'll aim to provide a straightforward explanation of the implications of this change. Let's make sure to keep things precise and user-friendly!
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

I recommend **removing `OfficerLockState.cs`**.

- **Why**: it’s currently **unused**, and the cases already delegate cleanly to `OfficerCaseLocking` while keeping EF-mapped scalar properties unchanged. Keeping an unused wrapper just adds noise/warnings and invites drift (two APIs to maintain).
- **When I’d keep it**: only if you explicitly want a “value-object style” API everywhere (i.e., refactor all 4 cases to store/use `OfficerLockState` and potentially map it as an EF owned type later).

If you want, I can remove `OfficerLockState.cs` and update references (should be none besides the file itself), then rerun `dotnet test`.

---

_**User**_

ok clean it then - can you look at the report of the compiler or resharper report and implement what they say? 

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Plan: I’ll remove the unused `OfficerLockState.cs`, then run a build/tests and scan the resulting warnings (and the IDE diagnostics) so we can address the concrete items ReSharper/compiler is flagging—without doing a noisy “delete all unused” sweep.

### Can I “execute a command” to see those ReSharper reports?
Not with what’s currently installed: **`jb` (JetBrains CLI) isn’t available**, and there’s no ReSharper command-line tool in your `dotnet tool` list. That’s why `dotnet build` shows **0 warnings** even though ReSharper shows many inspections.

### Best way to get me a machine-readable ReSharper report
- **In Rider**: run **Inspect Code** on the solution and export the report (HTML/XML).
- **In Visual Studio + ReSharper**: use **ReSharper → Inspect → Code Issues in Solution** and export.

If you paste the **exported report** (or attach the file), I can implement exactly what it recommends.

### If you want it fully command-line
We can install **JetBrains ReSharper Command Line Tools** and run `jb inspectcode`, but that’s an install step.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Updating code structure**

I'm planning to update the code to use `string?` to ensure more flexibility. In the handler, I’ll set `var safe = value ?? string.Empty;` and invoke callbacks accordingly, aligning with `MudTextField`, which utilizes `EventCallback<string?>`. I’ll change `ValueChanged` and `OnSearch` to `EventCallback<string?>`, and ensure `Value` is set to `string?` as well. I want to have safe defaults to avoid breaking existing callers while applying necessary patches sequentially. Let's also suppress path errors in `App.razor` with ReSharper directives.
</details></think>

### Yes — I can now see them via `jb inspectcode`
- You installed `jetbrains.resharper.globaltools`, but `jb` wasn’t on PATH for the shell. I ran it directly from `~/.dotnet/tools/jb`.
- Your repo didn’t have a `.sln`, so I generated `SchaerbeekMunicipality.sln` and added all projects, then ran `jb inspectcode` to produce SARIF.

### What I fixed from the ReSharper/inspectcode “errors”
The initial `inspectcode` run showed several **nullability mismatches (CS8620/CS8622)** that your screenshot also hinted at. I fixed those by making handlers accept nullable inputs where the UI components can legitimately pass null:

- **MudFileUpload callbacks** now accept `IBrowserFile?`:
  - `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/BirthDeclaration/Components/MedicalDeclarationUpload.razor`
  - `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/IdentityDocuments/Components/ApplicantPhotoUpload.razor`
  - `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Components/DocumentUpload.razor`
  - `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/ChangeOfAddress/Pages/ChangeOfAddressCaseDetail.razor` (`UploadDocument(IBrowserFile? file)` + null guard)

- **Search callbacks** now accept `string?` where Mud components may pass null:
  - `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Municipal/Components/BelgianAddressFields.razor` (`Func<string?, ...> SearchStreets`)
  - `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/ChangeOfAddress/Pages/ChangeOfAddressCaseDetail.razor` (`SearchStreets(string? ...)`)
  - `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/Registration/Components/AddressStep.razor` (`SearchStreets(string? ...)`)
  - `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/DesignSystem/Components/Forms/AppSearchBar.razor` now uses nullable-friendly callbacks and normalizes null to `""`.

- **List page search handlers** now match `Action<string?>`:
  - `BirthDeclarationCaseList.razor`, `ChangeOfAddressCaseList.razor`, `DocumentRequestCaseList.razor`, `RegistrationCaseList.razor` (`OnSearch(string? _)`)

### What I fixed from the ReSharper warnings in your screenshot
- **False-positive static asset path warnings** for Blazor static web assets (`_content/MudBlazor/...`) by scoping a suppression in:
  - `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Components/App.razor` (`Html.PathError`)

- **AccessToDisposedClosure** in integration tests by avoiding capturing `scope.ServiceProvider` inside lambdas:
  - `tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/RoleBoundariesAndCaseLockingTests.cs`

- **CheckNamespace** where the “wrong” namespace is intentional (Aspire template style) by suppressing it locally:
  - `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.ServiceDefaults/Extensions.cs`

### Verification
- `jb inspectcode` (warnings-only run) now shows **no `"level": "error"`** items.
- `dotnet test SchaerbeekMunicipality.sln` ✅ all tests pass.

If you want, I can keep going on **remaining warnings** (there are many pre-existing ones like naming, redundant usings, nullable-contract conditions, etc.). Tell me whether you want to target **only warnings introduced by recent changes**, or you want a broader “clean all warnings” sweep (which will touch a lot of files).

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import json
from collections import Counter
with open('resharper-current.sarif') as f:
    data = json.load(f)
results = data['runs'][0]['results']
print('Total issues:', len(results))
levels = Counter(r.get('level','?') for r in results)
print('By level:', dict(levels))
rules = Counter(r['ruleId'] for r in results)
print('\nTop 25 rules:')
for rule, count in rules.most_common(25):
    print(f'  {count:4d}  {rule}')
PY
```

The "using directive is not required" warnings are gone from inspectcode. The IDE may still show cached warnings, or other warnings may remain.

The IDE likely shows stale warnings or different severity thresholds. I will extract all fixable warnings from the SARIF and apply automated fixes. Safe categories include redundant suppressions, unused variables, and switch pattern merges.

Safe fixes cover init-only properties, private members, unused parameters, and naming conventions. Unsafe warnings include unused global members, positional property serialization, and unused method return values. These should be suppressed or ignored rather than auto-fixed.

NotAccessedPositionalProperty warnings on DTOs are false positives caused by JSON serialization. I will create a suppress file for those. A script will parse the SARIF to fix all remaining fixable warnings automatically.

I will try running cleanupcode with a more aggressive profile. I will also check the solution archive for an existing ReSharper settings file.

```bash
import json
from collections import Counter
with open('resharper-current.sarif') as f:
    data = json.load(f)
results = data['runs'][0]['results']
files = Counter()
for r in results:
    if r['ruleId'] == 'NotAccessedPositionalProperty.Global':
        uri = r['locations'][0]['physicalLocation']['artifactLocation']['uri']
        files[uri.split('/')[-1]] += 1
print('Top files for NotAccessedPositionalProperty:')
for f,c in files.most_common(15):
    print(c, f)
PY
```

```bash
import json, re
from collections import defaultdict
with open('resharper-current.sarif') as f:
    results = json.load(f)['runs'][0]['results']

def path(r):
    return r['locations'][0]['physicalLocation']['artifactLocation']['uri'].replace('file:///Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/','')

groups = defaultdict(list)
for r in results:
    if r['ruleId'] == 'RedundantSuppressNullableWarningExpression':
        loc = r['locations'][0]['physicalLocation']['region']
        groups[path(r)].append(loc['startLine'])

for p, lines in sorted(groups.items()):
    print(p, sorted(set(lines)))
PY
```

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path

root = Path('/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality')

# Remove unused openHandler declarations (line numbers from inspectcode)
removals = {
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/AddressAndHouseholdTests.cs': [20, 37],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/CaseDecisionTests.cs': [24, 118],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/CaseIntakeTests.cs': [43],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/CertificatesAndNotificationsTests.cs': [29],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/IntakeCorrectionTests.cs': [166],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/NationalRegisterTests.cs': [92, 112, 137],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/PoliceVerificationTests.cs': [19],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/ResidenceCategoryTests.cs': [108],
}

for rel, lines in removals.items():
    path = root / rel
    content = path.read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
    for line_no in sorted(lines, reverse=True):
        idx = line_no - 1
        if 'openHandler' not in content[idx]:
            raise SystemExit(f'Expected openHandler at {rel}:{line_no}, got: {content[idx]!r}')
        del content[idx]
    path.write_text(''.join(content))
    print('fixed', rel)

# Remove unused static fields in domain tests
field_removals = {
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain.Tests/Registration/RegistrationCaseAddressTests.cs': [10, 11],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain.Tests/Registration/RegistrationCaseCorrectionTests.cs': [10, 11],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain.Tests/Registration/RegistrationCaseResidenceTests.cs': [11, 12],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain.Tests/Registration/RegistrationCasePoliceVerificationTests.cs': [11],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain.Tests/NationalRegister/RegistrationCaseNationalRegisterTests.cs': [11, 14],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain.Tests/IdentityDocuments/DocumentRequestCaseTests.cs': [13],
}

for rel, lines in field_removals.items():
    path = root / rel
    content = path.read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
    for line_no in sorted(lines, reverse=True):
        del content[line_no - 1]
    path.write_text(''.join(content))
    print('fixed fields', rel)
PY
```

    &#39;tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/BirthDeclaration/BirthDeclarationTests.cs&#39;: [47, 48, 72],
    &#39;tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/ChangeOfAddress/ChangeOfAddressTests.cs&#39;: [103],
    &#39;tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/IdentityDocuments/DocumentRequestTests.cs&#39;: [181],

for rel, lines in files_lines.items():
    path = root / rel
    content = path.read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
    for line_no in lines:

        content[idx] = new
        print(f&#39;{rel}:{line_no}\n  {old.rstrip()}\n  {new.rstrip()}&#39;)
    path.write_text(&#39;&#39;.join(content))
PY</summary>

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import re
from pathlib import Path

root = Path('/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality')

files_lines = {
    'src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Api/Middleware/DemoOfficerMiddleware.cs': [16],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.E2E.Tests/Infrastructure/MunicipalWebHost.cs': [45],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.E2E.Tests/Journeys/BirthDeclarationE2ETests.cs': [43],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/BirthDeclaration/BirthDeclarationTests.cs': [47, 48, 72],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/ChangeOfAddress/ChangeOfAddressTests.cs': [103],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/IdentityDocuments/DocumentRequestTests.cs': [181],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/PersonFile/PersonFileTests.cs': [88, 94],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/CaseIntakeTests.cs': [29],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/PoliceVerificationTests.cs': [156],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/RegistrationApiTests.cs': [41, 106],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/ResidenceCategoryTests.cs': [86],
}

for rel, lines in files_lines.items():
    path = root / rel
    content = path.read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
    for line_no in lines:
        idx = line_no - 1
        old = content[idx]
        new = re.sub(r'(\w+)!\b', r'\1', old)
        if old == new:
            new = old.replace('!', '')
        content[idx] = new
        print(f'{rel}:{line_no}\n  {old.rstrip()}\n  {new.rstrip()}')
    path.write_text(''.join(content))
PY
```

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
root = Path('/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality')

# Remove redundant OpenRegistrationCaseHandler usings after openHandler removal
using_to_remove = 'using SchaerbeekMunicipality.Application.Features.Registration.OpenRegistrationCase;\n'
files = [
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain.Tests/IdentityDocuments/DocumentRequestCaseTests.cs',
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Domain.Tests/NationalRegister/RegistrationCaseNationalRegisterTests.cs',
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/AddressAndHouseholdTests.cs',
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/CaseDecisionTests.cs',
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/NationalRegisterTests.cs',
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/PoliceVerificationTests.cs',
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/ResidenceCategoryTests.cs',
]
for rel in files:
    path = root / rel
    text = path.read_text()
    if using_to_remove in text:
        path.write_text(text.replace(using_to_remove, ''))
        print('removed using from', rel)

removals = {
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/PoliceVerificationTests.cs': [46, 77, 100, 141],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/CaseDecisionTests.cs': [28, 120],
    'tests/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Integration.Tests/Features/Registration/CertificatesAndNotificationsTests.cs': [34],
}
for rel, lines in removals.items():
    path = root / rel
    content = path.read_text().splitlines(keepends=True)
    for line_no in sorted(lines, reverse=True):
        if 'Handler' not in content[line_no-1]:
            raise SystemExit(f'unexpected line {rel}:{line_no}: {content[line_no-1]!r}')
        del content[line_no-1]
    path.write_text(''.join(content))
    print('removed unused handlers from', rel)
PY
```
